code: games_r <- lapply(1:4, function(s) tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = s)) games_g <- lapply(1:4, function(s) tsr_play_game("greedy", "random", seed = s)) p_random <- tsr_behavior_profile(games_r, side = 1L, label = "X random") p_greedy <- tsr_behavior_profile(games_g, side = 1L, label = "X greedy") tsr_compare_profiles(p_random, p_greedy) #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>   label     side n_games n_moves_total accuracy mean_regret blunder_rate #>   <chr>    <int>   <int>         <int>    <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 X random     1       4           177    0          0.376        0.870  #> 2 X greedy     1       4            96    0.125      0.0197       0.0833 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: win_conversion <dbl>, defense_rate <dbl>, #> #   aggression <dbl>, mean_decisiveness <dbl>

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: tesseractR plays tic-tac-toe four-dimensional 4×4×4×4 hypercube — 256 cells indexed (, j, k, l) coordinate 0:3. line four cells whose successive differences equal constant non-zero direction vector {−1, 0, 1}^4. deduplicating reversed directions 520 winning lines standard ((n+2)^d − n^d) / 2 formula d = n = 4.

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, 0L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X at (0,0,0,0) = cell 1 b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 17L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 1L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X at (1,0,0,0) = cell 2 print(b) #>  #> ── <ttt_board> ── #>  #> 4x4x4x4 board, 256 cells #> moves played: 3 #> to move: O #> legal moves: 253 tsr_plot(b)

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: continue game X completes axis-aligned line:
code: b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 18L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 2L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 19L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 3L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X completes 1-2-3-4 tsr_check_win(b) #> [1] 1 tsr_winning_line(b) #> [1] 1 2 3 4 tsr_plot_slice(b, axis = "l", at = 0L)

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: tsr_ai_move() returns chosen linear cell index. Difficulty 1 always takes immediate win blocks immediate threat. Higher difficulties look ahead (search exponential pure R — depth 4 may take several seconds).
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 1L)        # human X ai_cell <- tsr_ai_move(b, difficulty = 1L) b <- tsr_move(b, cell = ai_cell)   # AI O ai_cell #> [1] 4

code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 1L); b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 17L) b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 2L) tsr_win_prob(b, player = 1L) #> [1] 0.5502794 head(tsr_rate_moves(b)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>    cell     i     j     k     l score win_prob  rank is_best is_winning #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <dbl>    <dbl> <int> <lgl>   <lgl>      #> 1     4     3     0     0     0     1    0.502     1 TRUE    FALSE      #> 2    86     1     1     1     1     0    0.5       2 FALSE   FALSE      #> 3   155     2     2     1     2     0    0.5       3 FALSE   FALSE      #> 4     3     2     0     0     0    -6    0.487     4 FALSE   FALSE      #> 5    20     3     0     1     0    -7    0.485     5 FALSE   FALSE      #> 6    22     1     1     1     0    -7    0.485     6 FALSE   FALSE      #> # ℹ 1 more variable: is_blocking <lgl>

title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
text: "heuristic" (default, real-time): maps tsr_evaluate() logistic calibration p = plogis((score - ) / b). coefficients , b fit data-raw/ script (S6) stored internal package data; provisional defaults used calibration run. "rollout" (offline accuracy): runs n policy-guided Monte-Carlo playouts using light heuristic policy returns empirical win share. Slower pure R; intended offline analysis, live UI. Terminal positions short-circuit: win returns 1, loss 0, draw 0.5.

title: tesseractR — Complete Build Roadmap
text: board 4×4×4×4. cell coordinates (, j, k, l), 0:3. Linear index: idx = 1 + + 4*j + 16*k + 64*l, idx ∈ 1:256. column-major flattening; keep consistent everywhere. Line directions: direction vector d ∈ {-1,0,1}^4, excluding zero vector. Normalize first non-zero component positive (dedups d -d). Count: (3^4 - 1) / 2 = 40 direction classes. Lines: direction d valid start cell s s + 3*d stays within 0:3 every axis, four cells s, s+d, s+2d, s+3d form line. Deduplicate lines (line reverse set). canonical total exactly 272 lines 4×4×4×4. title: tesseractR — Complete Build Roadmap
text: fully vectorized. Use integer arithmetic throughout.
code: .tsr_coord_to_idx <- function(i, j, k, l) {   # vectorized; inputs in 0:3; returns integer in 1:256   as.integer(1L + i + 4L * j + 16L * k + 64L * l) }  .tsr_idx_to_coord <- function(idx) {   # inverse; returns a matrix or data structure with columns i, j, k, l in 0:3   z <- idx - 1L   i <- z %% 4L   j <- (z %/% 4L) %% 4L   k <- (z %/% 16L) %% 4L   l <- (z %/% 64L) %% 4L   cbind(i = i, j = j, k = k, l = l) }

Axis ticks labelled 0:3 three free dimensions; scene aspect locked cube (scene = list(aspectmode = "cube")). Guard requireNamespace("plotly", quietly = TRUE); absent, fall back 2D tsr_plot_slice() render show inline note suggesting install.packages("plotly").

title: tesseractR — Complete Build Roadmap
code: # tesseractR 0.1.0  ## Initial release  * Game engine for 4D tic-tac-toe on a 4x4x4x4 hypercube with win detection   across all 272 winning lines (`tsr_new_board()`, `tsr_move()`, `tsr_undo()`,   `tsr_legal_moves()`, `tsr_check_win()`, `tsr_winning_line()`, `tsr_is_full()`,   `tsr_status()`). * Depth-limited negamax AI opponent with adjustable difficulty (`tsr_ai_move()`). * Position evaluation and calibrated win probability (`tsr_evaluate()`,   `tsr_win_prob()`), plus per-move ratings identifying the best next move   (`tsr_rate_moves()`). * Self-play simulation and opening/strategy statistics (`tsr_play_game()`,   `tsr_simulate()`, `tsr_opening_stats()`). * Game analysis and play-behavior analytics: move-by-move evaluation, turning   points, missed wins/blocks, regret, and behavioral profiling   (`tsr_analyze_game()`, `tsr_turning_points()`, `tsr_game_summary()`,   `tsr_behavior_profile()`, `tsr_compare_profiles()`, `tsr_plot_winprob()`). * ggplot2 visualization of the hypercube as a grid of boards (`tsr_plot()`,   `autoplot()` method), plus a 2D slice view fixing one axis (`tsr_plot_slice()`)   and a 3D plotly cube view in the app. * Interactive Shiny application with real-time move evaluation and a game-analysis   panel (`tsr_run_app()`).
